channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: HItman Classic Trilogy Remastered announced for next year
item.content:encoded:
Saber Interactive and IO Interactive have announced Hitman Classic Trilogy Remastered, which will be coming to PS5, Xbox Series X|S and PC in 2027.

Now, not to insult your intelligence or anything, but the Hitman Classic Trilogy will include Hitman: Codename 47, HItman 2: Silent Assassin and Hitman: Contracts. All three are being given a visual enhancements with upgraded character models, enhanced environments and high-resolution textures. If you want to HItman like it’s the year 2000, you can toggle seamlessly between the enhanced and the original graphics – which is always a great feature for these remasters. Saber are also building a photo mode into the game.

Check out some of the environmental transformations:

Compared to the more recent trilogy of HItman, IO Interactive were still very much figuring out what Hitman was through their first three games. Codename 47 brought an experimental tone, compared to the greater stealth focus of the second and a darker tone to the third game. And actually, Contracts was kind of a remake of the Codename 47, itself bringing more advanced graphics (from 2004 to the 2000 original), enhanced gameplay, but then weaving a new story and some additional levels into the mix. It’s a slight shame that this isn’t a quadrilogy to also include Hitman: Blood Money, the fourth game and last until 2012’s Hitman Absolution. In fact, the last time these games were remastered, back with 2013’s Hitman HD Trilogy for PS3 and Xbox 360, it was Silent Assassin, Contracts and Blood Money that were spruced up. Then again, Blood Money and Absolution are already playable on modern consoles, thanks to PS4 and Xbox One ports.

If you think that all sounds a bit confused and messy, let me remind you that there have been about 17 different ways that you can buy the 2016-2021 World of Assassination trilogy.

Saber has been busy in recent years, partnering on a number of remaster projects of this ilk, including Tomb Raider I-III Remastered, Tomb Raider IV-VI Remastered and Legacy of Kain Soul Reaver 1 & 2 Remastered.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/06/hitman-classic-trilogy-remastered-announced-for-next-year/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fields of Mistria 1.0 launch is coming on 5th August
item.content:encoded:
NPC Studio has announced that their charming life sim Fields of Mistria will be exiting early access with a 1.0 launch on 5th August 2026.

This launch date is two years to the day since Fields of Mistria entered into Early Access, bringing a magical twist to the Stardew-like cosy life sim set up. Mistria is a village tucked between the forest and the sea, and as you get to work building and customising your own homestead, growing crops, looking after animals and the like. But the town is thrown into disarray after an earthquake, and you need to help rebuild… except now a strange magic has start to flow through the land.

It’s a lovely game that has been very enjoyable through early access already, and with the 1.0 release, NPC Studio will be rounding out the experience…. and starting to shift their focus to what comes after.

Villagers & Romance:

  • NPC Heart caps raised to 10 Hearts
  • 10 Hearts Proposal & Wedding Events for romanceable NPCs
  • Marriage & Children
  • Stillwell & Zorel join the Saturday Market

Mines & Dungeons:

  • Additional Dungeon Quests
  • Additional Dungeon rewards

Town & Renown:

  • Renown Level cap raised to 100
  • Additional Town Repair Questline events
  • Town Repair Storyline is completable
  • Additional Requests added to the Request Board.
  • Additional Museum sets and rewards.

Skills & Perks:

  • Additional Skill Perks

Customization & Cosmetics:

  • Additional furniture and decor
  • Additional cosmetics
  • Additional mount skins

Misc.

  • Achievements
  • First wave Localization Languages (Beta versions):
  • Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), French, Japanese, Korean, Russian, Spanish
  • Balancing & Optimization
  • …and more!

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/06/fields-of-mistria-1-0-launch-is-coming-on-5th-august/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Lou’s Lagoon has got an August 27th release date
item.content:encoded:
Do you fancy a trip to some cosy islands this summer? Well, even if you aren’t planning a real getaway you can still live out a cosy trip through Lou’s Lagoon which will be launching on August 27th. You can even get a taster of this summer trip as a demo of Lou’s Lagoon is available on PC through Steam and Xbox PC.

Lou’s Lagoon sees players ending up in the Limbo archipelago following a storm, with player’s finding that Uncle Lou is missing. Players can hop into their seaplane to explore the archipelago, gathering different resources, and helping rebuild communities. Players will be able to create their own avatars and use various gadgets to gather the resources and go fishing too. If you play the Lou’s Lagoon demo and complete it you will get an exclusive skin for the full release.

Lou’s Lagoon will be available for PC, PS5, Xbox Series XS, and Nintendo Switch.

Source: Press Release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/06/lous-lagoon-has-got-an-august-27th-release-date/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
